key.name: tile-new-terminal
key.summary: Spawn / add a terminal to the focused monitor's batch, or summon it
key.description: Opens a terminal full-screen on the focused monitor, or adds another and re-divides the monitor into equal vertical columns. If a stray terminal is focused, it is absorbed into the batch instead. If the monitor already has a batch but it is not the focused window, the whole group is raised to the front and focused instead.

key.name: minimize-terminal-group
key.summary: Toggle (minimise / restore) the focused monitor's terminal batch
key.description: If any terminal in the batch on the focused window's monitor is still open, minimises the whole group at once; if they are all minimised, brings them all back to the front and focuses them. The batch also minimises and restores as a unit when any single member is minimised or restored.

key.name: move-terminal-left
key.summary: Move the focused terminal one position towards the start of its group
key.description: If the focused window belongs to a monitor's batch, swaps it with the previous member (the column to its left, or the row above it) and re-flows the group. Does nothing if it is already first or not in a batch.

key.name: move-terminal-right
key.summary: Move the focused terminal one position towards the end of its group
key.description: If the focused window belongs to a monitor's batch, swaps it with the next member (the column to its right, or the row below it) and re-flows the group. Does nothing if it is already last or not in a batch.

key.name: maximize-terminal
key.summary: Maximise the focused terminal over its group (hide the column division)
key.description: Grows the focused terminal to fill the whole monitor work area, covering its column peers, so one terminal is temporarily full-screen without leaving the batch. The peers stay alive behind it; the division comes back via unmaximize-terminal, or whenever the group is minimised.

key.name: unmaximize-terminal
key.summary: Restore the focused monitor's terminal batch to its column division
key.description: Undoes maximize-terminal: re-flows the batch on the focused window's monitor back into equal columns/rows. Does nothing if the monitor's batch is not currently maximised.

key.name: terminal-command
key.summary: Command used to launch a new terminal
key.description: Parsed with shell quoting rules and spawned when the keybinding needs a new terminal window.

key.name: terminal-wm-class
key.summary: Substring matched (case-insensitive) against a window's WM_CLASS to decide whether it is a terminal
key.description: Used both to recognise a newly spawned terminal window and to decide whether a focused window should be absorbed into the batch.

key.name: orientation
key.description: 'columns' = side-by-side vertical splits (full height each). 'rows' = stacked horizontal splits (full width each).
